source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure Workdocs access is limited to a range of allowable IP addresses

input_row.source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
input_row.title: Ensure Workdocs access is limited to a range of allowable IP addresses
input_row.description: Access to WorkDocs can be limited to an allowed range of IP addresses.
input_row.rationale: Using IP address allow lists, you define and permit access to your WorkDocs site from trusted networks.
input_row.audit: Perform these steps to review the list of IP addresses allowed to access WorkDocs From the Console: 1. Log into the AWS console. 2. Navigate to WorkDocs or go to WorkDocs Console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/zocalo/ 3. Under My Account, choose Open admin control panel. 4. For IP Allow List, choose Change. 5. Review the IP address ranges and any single IP addresses 6. Click Cancel. If the IP address ranges do not match trusted networks refer to the remediation below to create or edit the IP Allow list.
input_row.remediation: Perform the steps below to create or edit the IP Allow list for WorkDocs From the Console: 1. Log into the AWS console. 2. Navigate to WorkDocs or go to WorkDocs Console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/zocalo/ 3. Under My Account, choose Open admin control panel. 4. For IP Allow List, choose Change. Internal Only - General 5. For Enter CIDR value, enter the IP address ranges to allowlist. To allow access from a single IP address, specify /32 as the CIDR prefix. 6. Click Add. 7. Click Save Changes.
input_row.impact: IP Lists currently only work for IPv4 addresses and denying access through an IP list is not supported.
input_row.justification: AWS WorkDocs does not currently expose IP restriction settings via AWS APIs, SDKs (such as boto3), AWS CLI, or AWS Config. The configuration for allowable IP addresses is managed within the WorkDocs administrative console and is not accessible for direct programmatic inspection or enforcement. Both AI opinions correctly note the lack of automation capability, with the second opinion suggesting indirect monitoring via AWS Config or CloudTrail; however, these services do not provide visibility into the specific IP restriction settings required by this control.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. Log in to the AWS Management Console and navigate to the WorkDocs service. 2. Select the relevant WorkDocs site and access the administrative settings. 3. Locate the section for network or access control settings (often under "Site settings" or "Access controls"). 4. Review the configuration for IP address restrictions and verify that only the approved range(s) of IP addresses are permitted. 5. Confirm that the configuration matches organizational policy and that no unauthorized IP ranges are allowed. 6. Document your findings, including screenshots or configuration details as evidence of compliance.
input_row.default_value: By default, no IP addresses are allowed.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The rationale clearly states that AWS WorkDocs does not expose IP restriction settings via AWS APIs, SDKs, AWS CLI, or AWS Config.
- The audit steps require human interaction within the AWS Console to access and verify IP allow lists.
- The control focuses on application-level settings rather than a standard AWS service property, which is typically API-queryable.
- There are no AWS CLI commands or boto3 methods available for querying IP allow lists in WorkDocs.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Log in to the AWS Management Console and navigate to the WorkDocs service.
2. Select the relevant WorkDocs site and access the administrative settings.
3. Locate the section for network or access control settings (often under "Site settings" or "Access controls").
4. Review the configuration for IP address restrictions and verify that only the approved range(s) of IP addresses are permitted.
5. Confirm that the configuration matches organizational policy and that no unauthorized IP ranges are allowed.
6. Document your findings, including screenshots or configuration details as evidence of compliance.
